RAILROADS NOT in


POLITICS, SAYS HILL

pages.raw_text:
SEATTLE Dec. 2G.-PreSident ], ].
Hill of the Great Northern was in
terviewed just before leaving Seattle
on politics.


1T is very grating ne to hear
that the Great Northern is in poll
tics said Mir. Hill. 1T never has
been and never will be So long as
can prevent il. Whay we want is to
be given assistance in the work we
are trying to do for Seattle and the
state of Washington. and not to be
hampered. When the lines OF the
Northern Securities company in this
part of the country prosper SO will
Seattle, and when we are experiencing
hard times then the effect VIII be
felt here.


Naturally ! am opposed to the
railroad commission bill. for it will
interfere with our plans.


AT the present time we are putting
in freight rates that are far below
what will he profitable to us for the
very reason that We are trying to de-
veiop this oriental trade III order
to do this we must put III rates that
will give us the traffic. and it is to
our own interest as much as it is to
anyone else. There is no use in fight
